channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DOGE released data about federal contract savings. It doesn’t add up
item.description: A new government tracker claims DOGE has saved billions from ending federal contracts. But an NPR analysis of the data finds the claimed savings don’t add up.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Texas Teacher Retirement System leader says Senate voucher proposal would not harm retirement funds
item.description: A recent budget analysis noted the Texas Senate’s voucher bill could affect the fund if it leads students and teachers to leave public schools.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy war of words heats up even as US looks to wind down war in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy accused President Donald Trump of perpetuating Russian “disinformation" on Wednesday, while Trump clapped back and labeled Zelenskyy a “dictator" who has failed his country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration identifies hundreds of energy projects that could be fast-tracked
item.description: The Trump administration has identified hundreds of “emergency” energy projects whose approvals could be fast-tracked. The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers has updated its website to put hundreds of new projects on its emergency docket under President Trump’s energy emergency declaration.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmaker who led state to require ethnic studies in schools decries effort to undo it
item.description: State Rep. Samantha Sencer-Mura, DFL-Minneapolis, authored the bill that set ethnic studies standards a few years ago. She joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer on Wednesday in response to Republican Rep. Ron Kresha’s legislation.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Cuts to Veterans Affairs made in 'dishonest' way: Former employee
item.description: Employees fired included researchers looking into cancer treatments, opioid addiction and burn pit exposure.
item.dc:creator: Nichole Berlie, NewsNation

item.title: HUD offices became as vacant as a ‘Spirit Halloween’ store under Biden: admin sources
item.description: D.C.'s Housing and Urban Development headquarters is among the federal buildings that turned into empty "Spirit Halloween" stores under the Biden administration, Fox Digital learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOJ and NYC mayor face judge's scrutiny in bid to dismiss criminal charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Justice Department lawyers and New York City Mayor Eric Adams are set to face a federal judge who is signaling that he’s unlikely to rubber stamp their request to drop the mayor's corruption charges weeks before an April trial. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy to step down, asks for replacement
item.description: "I have decided it is time to start the process of identifying my successor and of preparing the Postal Service for this change,” DeJoy said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: For a St. Cloud family, Trump’s pause of refugee admissions brings worry for Afghan relatives
item.description: One of President Trump’s first actions after taking office was an executive order indefinitely suspending the U.S. refugee resettlement program. A family living in St. Cloud who fled Afghanistan a year ago worries about relatives left behind.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Energy secretary blasts 'sinister' zero carbon emissions goal
item.description: “Net zero 2050 is a sinister goal, it’s a terrible goal,” Wright said to applause at the Alliance for Responsible Citizenship forum.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: How a South Texas community college embraced apprenticeships to ease a growing nursing shortage
item.description: South Texas College in McAllen launched one of the first registered nursing apprenticeships in the country as area hospitals expect the need for nurses to increase.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Sneha Dey, KPRC 2

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspended Hillsborough Democrat defends online posts some say are antisemitic
item.description: The Hillsborough County Democratic Executive Committee has suspended a member until January 2027 over posts online and wording in email messages.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks Trump’s gender-affirming care executive orders nationwide
item.description: BALTIMORE — A federal judge Thursday blocked parts of two executive orders issued by President Trump that collectively seek to restrict gender-affirming care. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WFLA, NBC
